title: 第 96 節
en: 4. He who knows the Rik verses and the letters n and sh for every Sawhita, he knows the Sa;/zhita with strength and breath. Let him know that this is the life of the Sawhita.

5. If the pupil asks, 'Shall I say it with the letter n or without it ? ' let the teacher say, ' With the letter iu And if he asks, ' Shall I say it with the letter sh or without it?' let the teacher say, 'With the letter sh V 6. Hrasva Ma;^ukeya said : ' If we here recite the verses according to the Sa;;zhita (attending to the necessary changes of n and s into n and sh 2), and if we say the adhyaya of Ma^iikeya (Ait. Ar. Ill, 1), then the letters n and sh (strength and breath) have by this been obtained for us.'
7. Sthavira .Sakalya said : ' If we recite the verses according to the Sawhita, and if we say the adhyaya of Ma?^ukeya, then the letters n and sh have by this been obtained for us.'
8. Here the Rishis, the Kavasheyas3, knowing 1 The letters n and sh refer most likely to the rules of watva and shatva, i. e. the changing of n and s into n and sh.
2 If we know whenever n and s should be changed to n and sh in the Sawhita.

3 The Kavasheyas said that, after they had arrived at the highest knowledge of Brahman (through the various forms of meditation and worship that lead to it and that have been described in the Upanishad) no further meditation and no further sacrifice could be 266 AITAREYA-ARAiVYAKA.

this, said : ' Why should we repeat (the Veda), why should we sacrifice ? We offer as a sacrifice breath in speech, or speech in breath. What is the beginning (of one), that is the end (of the other).'

9. Let no one tell these Sa/whitas (Ait. Ar. Ill, 1III, 2) to one who is not a resident pupil, who has not been with his teacher at least one year, and who is not himself to become an instructor \ Thus say the teachers, yea, thus say the teachers.

required. Instead of the morning and evening stoma they offer breath in speech, whenever they speak, or speech in breath, when they are silent or asleep. When speech begins, breathing ceases ; when breathing begins, speech ceases.

1 The strict prohibition uttered at the end of the third Arawyaka, not to divulge a knowledge of the Sawmita-upanishad (Ait. Ar. Ill, 1-2), as here explained, is peculiar. It would have seemed self-evident that, like the rest of the miti or sacred literature, the A Arawyaka too, and every portion of it, could have been learnt from the mouth of a teacher only, and according to rule (niyamena), i. e. by a pupil performing all the duties of a student (brahmaMrin2), so that no one except a regular pupil (antevasin) could possibly gain access to it. Nor can there be any doubt that we ought to take the words asa?/zvatsaravasin and apravaktrz as limitations, and to translate, ' Let no one tell these Sawhitas to any pupil who has not at least been a year with his master, and who does not mean to become a teacher in turn.'

That this is the right view is confirmed by similar injunctions given at the end of the fifth Ara?/yaka. Here we have first some rules as to who is qualified to recite the Mahavrata. No one is permitted to do so, who has not passed through the Diksha, the initiation for the Agnish/oma. If the Mahavrata is performed as a Sattra, the sacrificer is a Hotrz' priest, and he naturally has passed through that ceremony. But if the Mahavrata is performed as an Ekaha or Ahina ceremony, anybody might be the sacrificer, and therefore it was necessary to say that no one who is adikshita, uninitiated, should recite it for another person ; nor should he do so, 2 Apastamba-sutras, translated by Buhler, p. 18.

Ill ARAiVYAKA, 2 ADHYAYA, 6 KHANDA. 267 when the Mahavrata is performed without (or with) an altar, or if it does not last one year. In saying, however, that one should not recite the Mahavrata for another person, parents and teachers are not to be understood as included, because what is done for them, is done for ourselves.
After these restrictions as to the recitation of the Mahavrata, follow other restrictions as to the teaching of it, and here we read, as at the end of the Upanishad :

4. ' Let no one teach this day, the Mahavrata, to one who is not a regular pupil (antevasin), and has been so for one year, certainly not to one who has not been so for one year ; nor to one who is not a brahma^arin and does not study the same Veda \ certainly not to one who does not study the same Veda ; nor to one who does not come to him.
5. ' Let the teaching not be more than saying it once or twice, twice only.
6. ' One man should tell it to one man, so says £atukar«ya.
7. ' Not to a child, nor to a man in his third stage of life.
8. ' The teacher and pupil should not stand, nor walk, nor lie down, nor sit on a couch; but they should both sit on the ground.

9. 'The pupil should not lean backward while learning, nor lean forward. He should not be covered with too much clothing, nor assume the postures of a devotee, but without using any of the apparel of a devotee, simply elevate his knees. Nor should he learn, when he has eaten flesh, when he has seen blood, or a corpse, or when he has done an unlawful thing2; when he has anointed his eyes, oiled or rubbed his body, when he has been shaved or bathed, put colour on, or ornamented himself with flowerwreaths, when he has been writing or effacing his writing 3.

10. ' Nor should he finish the reading in one day, so says Gatukamya, while according to Galava, he should finish it in one day. Agnive-ryayana holds that he should finish all before the Tn'kajitis *, and then rest in another place finishing it.
11. 'And in the place where he reads this, he should not read 1 See Gautama-sutras XIV, 21, and Buhler's note.
2 Navratyam akramya is explained by the commentator by u££>feh/adyakrama#a.
3 This, if rightly translated, would seem to be the earliest mention of actual writing in Sanskrit literature.
4 See Ait. Ar. I, 4, 3, 1-4.
268 AITAREYA-AR AiVYAK A.
anything else, though he may read this (the Mahavrata) where he has read something else.
12. 'No one should bathe and become a snataka1 who does not read this. Even if he has read many other things, he should not become a snataka if he has not read this.
13. 'Nor should he forget it, and even if he should forget anything else, he should not forget this.
14. ' No, he should never forget this.
15. 'If he does not forget this, it will be enough for himself (or for acquiring a knowledge of the Self).
zh: 4. 誰若通曉梨俱吠陀（Rik）的詩句，以及在每一本《沙姆希塔》（Sawhita）中的字母 n 和 sh，誰就以力量與氣息來認識《沙姆希塔》。讓他知道，這就是《沙姆希塔》的生命。

5. 若弟子問：「我應該用字母 n 來念誦，還是不用？」則教師應說：「用字母 n。」若弟子問：「我應該用字母 sh 來念誦，還是不用？」則教師應說：「用字母 sh。」 6. 赫拉斯瓦‧馬尤克耶亞（Hrasva Ma;^ukeya）說：「若我們在此依照《梵經集》（Sa;;zhita）來誦讀頌詩（留意 n 與 s 變為 n 與 sh 的必要變化），且若我們誦讀馬尤克耶亞的《講章》（Ait. Ar. Ill, 1），則字母 n 與 sh（力量與呼吸）便已藉此為我們而獲得。」
斯塔維羅‧薩卡利亞（Sthavira Sakalya）說：「若我們按照《梵本合誦》（Swhita）誦讀詩句，若我們誦讀曼杜凱亞的《阿地雅亞》（adhyaya of Mandūkeya），則字母 n 和 sh 因此已為我們所得。」
8. 於此處，聖賢（Rishis）、卡瓦舍亞人（Kavasheyas）¹知曉 ¹ 字母 n 和 sh 最可能指涉 watva 和 shatva 的規則，即 n 和 s 變化為 n 和 sh。
2 如果我們知道在《梵文連誦》(Sawhita)中何時應將 n 和 s 改變為 n 和 sh。
卡瓦舍亞人說，在他們通過奧義書中所述的各種冥想與祭祀形式而達到了對梵天的最高知識之後，就不再需要進一步的冥想，也不再需要進一步的祭祀。
他們說：「我們何必反覆誦習《吠陀》，何必進行祭祀？我們以氣息祭祀言語，或以言語祭祀氣息。一者的開始即是另一者的終結。」
9. 勿向任何非住校弟子、未曾侍奉師傅至少一年、且本身非欲成為教導者之人宣說此等薩姆希塔經（Saṃhitā）（《愛因德羅耶經》III, 1III, 2）。教師如是言，誠然教師如是言。
此舉乃是必需的。他們不再獻祭晨昏時刻的香氣（stoma），而是獻祭言語中的呼吸，只要他們開口說話；或者獻祭呼吸中的言語，當他們沉默或睡眠之際。言語開始時，呼吸就停止；呼吸開始時，言語就停止。

第三《阿蘭亞卡》末尾所發出的嚴格禁令，禁止洩露《娑摩吠陀奧義書》（Ait. Ar. Ill, 1-2）的知識，如此解釋是特殊的。似乎本應不言自明，就如同其餘的彌提（miti）或聖典文獻一樣，《阿蘭亞卡》及其各個部分，都只能從師傅口中習得，並按照規則（niyamena）進行，亦即由一名學生執行學生的所有職責（brahmaMrin2），如此一來，除了正規弟子（antevasin）外，沒有任何人能夠獲得對它的接觸。並且毫無疑問，我們應當將「asaṃvatsaravasin」及「apravaktrṛ」這些詞彙視為限定詞，並譯為：「任何人都不應將這些《娑摩吠陀》傳授給任何未曾至少與其師傅居住一年之久、且不打算輪流成為教師之人。」

這一點得到了《阿蘭耶卡》（Ara?/yaka）第五卷末尾所載類似規定的證實。在此我們首先看到若干關於誰有資格誦唱《摩訶婆羅多》（Mahavrata）的規則。任何未經過《迪克沙》（Diksha）—— 即《阿格尼索瑪》（Agnish/oma）的入教儀式 —— 的人，都不被允許誦唱此經。若《摩訶婆羅多》以《薩特拉》（Sattra）的方式進行，祭祀者乃《荷特利》（Hotrz'）祭司，他自然已經通過了該儀式。然而，若《摩訶婆羅多》以《厄卡哈》（Ekaha）或《阿希那》（Ahina）儀式的方式進行，任何人都可能成為祭祀者，因此有必要規定，未經入教的人（adikshita）不應為他人誦唱此經；他也不應該…… --- 2 《阿帕斯坦巴經書》（Apastamba-sutras），布勒譯，第18頁。
《雅利安亞迦》第二篇章第六分卷 267 當摩訶縛剌多（Mahavrata）在沒有（或有）祭壇的情況下舉行，或若其持續時間未滿一年時。然而在說明人不應為他人誦習摩訶縛剌多時，父母與教師不應被理解為包含其中，因為為他們所做之事，乃為我們自己所做之事。
在這些關於《大願節》（Mahavrata）誦習的限制之後，接著是有關教授它的其他限制。在此我們讀到，正如在《奧義書》末尾所見的：
4. 「任何人不得在此日教授大誓（Mahavrata）予非正式門徒（antevasin）者，尤其是未滿一年者；亦不得教予非梵行者（brahmaçarin）且不研習同一吠陀者，實在不得教予不研習同一吠陀者；更不得教予不就學於彼者。」
5. 「教導應當只是說一次或兩次，只有兩次而已。
6. 「一人應當向一人述說此事，」如舍圖卡耶亞（Satukaraya）所言。
7. 『不傳授予兒童，亦不傳授予人生第三階段的人。』
8. 『教師與學生不應站立、行走、躺臥或坐在長沙發上；兩人都應坐在地上。
9. 「學生學習時不應向後靠，也不應向前傾。不應穿著過多的衣服，也不應採取苦行者的姿態，但應在不使用任何苦行者服飾的情況下，簡單地抬起膝蓋。他不應在進食過肉類、見過血液、屍體或做過非法之事時學習2；不應在塗油眼睛、油膩或摩擦身體、被剃鬚或沐浴、搽上色彩或用花冠裝飾自己、進行書寫或擦除其書寫時學習3。
10. 『他不應在一天內完成誦讀，根據葛圖卡密亞（Gatukamya）之說；但依葛拉瓦（Galava）之見，他應在一天內完成。阿格尼韋里亞那（Agniveryayana）主張他應在特里卡吉蒂斯（Tri'kajitis）*之前完成全部，爾後在另一處所安息而完成之。
11. 「在他誦讀此文的地方，他不應該誦讀」¹ ¹ 見《高塔馬經》（Gautama-sutras）XIV, 21，及布勒（Buhler）的註釋。
2 評論者將 Navratyam akramya 解釋為 uttarādhyakramaṇa。
3 倘若翻譯得當，這似乎是梵文文獻中對實際書寫最早的記載。
見《愛達羅耶奧義書》（Ait. Ar.）I, 4, 3, 1-4。
268 愛多雷亞-阿蘭亞卡
別的任何東西，雖然他可能在他已讀過別的東西的地方閱讀這部經典（Mahavrata）。
12. 『任何人若未誦讀此篇，即不應沐浴而成為斯那塔卡（snataka）。縱然他已誦讀許多其他典籍，若未誦讀此篇，亦不應成為斯那塔卡。』 1 編注：梵語 snataka，指完成吠陀學習並舉行沐浴儀式的年輕男性婆羅門。
13. 「他也不應當遺忘它，即便他應當遺忘其他任何事物，他也不應當遺忘這一點。
14. 「不，他永遠不應該忘記這一點。
15. 「如果他不忘記這一點，這對他本身就足夠了（或足以獲得對自我〔Self〕的知識）。」
